sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Understanding Momentum Shifts in (LTH)
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of Life Time Group Holdings Inc. (LTH) stock, highlighting stable neutral readings in shorter horizons that could suggest an easing of a long-term weak bias. It discusses resistance testing, an exceptional risk-reward short setup, and offers three institutional trading strategies tailored for different risk profiles, along with a multi-timeframe signal analysis. The piece also details various AI-generated signals and real-time analysis features for investors.

feed.title: Franklin Resources Inc Stock: Dividend Reliability and Strategic Expansion in Asset Management Amid Market Shifts
feed.summary: Franklin Resources Inc (NYSE:BEN) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.33 per share, payable April 10, 2026, offering an attractive annualized yield of nearly 5.8%. The company demonstrates long-term stability with 45 years of consecutive dividend payments and is strategically expanding into private markets and alternative investments to drive future growth. While its P/E ratio is currently above the sector average, fair value estimates suggest potential undervaluation, making it appealing to income and value investors.

feed.title: 03/31/2026: Starbucks Dress Code, Netflix Media Contact Rule
feed.summary: This article summarizes several NLRB decisions and advice memos from March 2026, covering various labor law topics. Key rulings include the dismissal of allegations against Starbucks regarding its dress code enforcement, the dismissal of a representation petition due to the successor-bar doctrine, and clarification on jurisdictional standards for symphony orchestras. Additionally, an advice memo determined that a Netflix employee's discharge for sharing confidential business information was lawful, and another memo found a collectively bargained harassment provision lawful under Magnavox.

feed.title: NiSource (NYSE:NI) Now Covered by KeyCorp
feed.summary: KeyCorp initiated coverage on NiSource (NYSE:NI) with an "overweight" rating and a $52.00 price target, implying an 11.7% upside. This follows NiSource's recent strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates and providing optimistic FY2026 guidance. The stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with institutional investors holding a significant stake.

feed.title: BTIG Research Lowers Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) Price Target to $131.00
feed.summary: BTIG Research has lowered its price target for Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) to $131.00 from $140.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential 28.6% upside. Several other analysts have also adjusted their targets, bringing the consensus price target to $135.68 with a "Moderate Buy" rating. The adjustment comes after Abbott reported Q4 EPS of $1.50 and revenue of $11.46 billion, slightly missing revenue expectations, and provided guidance for Q1 and FY 2026 EPS.

feed.title: After the close on April 14, Mama's Creations will break down Q4
feed.summary: Mama's Creations (NASDAQ: MAMA) is scheduled to release its fiscal fourth-quarter results for the period ending January 31, 2026, after the market closes on April 14, 2026, followed by an investor conference call at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time. The call will cover the Q4 FY2026 results, provide a corporate update, and include a Q&A session. Management plans to discuss the integration progress of its Crown 1 and Bay Shore acquisitions and reiterates its long-term objective of achieving $1 billion in deli prepared foods revenue by 2030.

feed.title: Pace of U.S. Small Business Employment Remains Consistent in March
feed.summary: The Paychex Small Business Employment Watch for March indicates stable job and wage growth among U.S. small businesses, with the Jobs Index slightly increasing to 98.81. Hourly earnings growth remained below three percent for the 17th consecutive month at 2.66%. Small businesses face challenges in finding qualified employees amidst a stable, low-fire, low-hire environment.

feed.title: Blue Point‑Backed National Safety Apparel Turns Up Protection with Addition of HPFR and HiViz Product Lines
feed.summary: Blue Point Capital Partners and its portfolio company, National Safety Apparel (NSA), have acquired Lakeland Industries’ High Performance Flame Resistant (HPFR) and High-Visibility (HiViz) product lines. This acquisition signifies the sixth add-on acquisition for NSA since partnering with Blue Point, significantly expanding NSA's offerings for industrial and utility workers. The move strengthens NSA's position as a leading provider of critical protective solutions and underscores Blue Point's strategy of building scale through complementary products and acquisitions.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin says new hub can cut defense prototyping to months
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin (LMT) has opened a Rapid Fielding Center in Dallas to significantly accelerate the development, testing, and production of prototypes for U.S. government customers, aiming to reduce timelines from years to months. This initiative is part of a larger investment strategy, including over $7 billion since the prior presidential term and a planned multibillion-dollar investment over the next three years to expand and modernize more than 20 facilities. The center is designed to integrate prototyping with manufacturing for immediate design feedback, fostering speed and agility in delivering advanced defense capabilities.

feed.title: As cyber attacks rise, Arch brings primary coverage to Canada
feed.summary: Arch Insurance North America is expanding its primary cyber coverage solution, Arch CyPro, into Canada starting April 1. This move aims to provide comprehensive cyber protection amidst a rise in cyber attacks, leveraging Arch's existing excess layer capabilities and specialized cyber risk engineering expertise. The service will be distributed through Arch Insurance Canada’s broker network and will offer French language options for Quebec-domiciled businesses.

feed.title: From rejected category to modern 4PL: Redwood’s path through Gartner analysis
feed.summary: Redwood Logistics successfully navigated a five-year journey with Gartner, leading to its recognition in the Magic Quadrant for modern 4PLs after an initial concept rejection. This validation has significantly reshaped the company's enterprise freight business strategy and provided crucial credibility for securing large contracts. The article highlights the importance of analyst validation as institutional cover for multi-million-dollar technology purchases and emphasizes a combined-arms approach integrating analyst relations with PR and demand generation.

feed.title: Gen Z Driving the Shift from Cards to Digital Wallets, New Research from Global Payments Suggests
feed.summary: Global Payments' 11th annual Global Payments Report reveals that while Americans still prefer plastic cards, younger generations, particularly Gen Z, are increasingly adopting digital wallets. The research forecasts a significant shift from direct card usage to digital wallets for both online and in-store spending in the U.S. by 2030, mirroring global trends where digital wallets already dominate. This generational shift is set to accelerate mobile-first payment preferences in everyday commerce.

feed.title: Genuine Parts sets April 21 earnings release, 8:30 a.m. ET call
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Company (NYSE: GPC) will report its first quarter 2026 financial results on April 21, 2026. The company will host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET following the release, with public access available via webcast or phone. A replay of the call will also be accessible two hours after its completion.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Launches Thermo Scientific Glacios 3 Cryo-TEM
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has introduced the Glacios 3 Cryo-TEM, a next-generation cryogenic transmission electron microscope equipped with the READY System. This new instrument aims to make structural biology research more accessible by enabling installation in a broader range of lab environments and improving throughput and data quality through AI-powered software. It is expected to accelerate therapeutic development by allowing more institutions and researchers to utilize cryo-EM technology.

feed.title: Iran Tensions And Helium Risks Put KLA Supply Chain In Focus
feed.summary: Geopolitical tensions arising from the Iran war are raising concerns over semiconductor supply chains, specifically regarding the supply of helium, a crucial material for chip manufacturing. KLA Corporation (NasdaqGS:KLAC), a key supplier to chip fabs, is highly exposed to these potential disruptions, which could impact demand for its inspection and process control tools. Investors should monitor customer commentary, KLA's order mix, and updates on tariffs and sourcing strategies to assess the company's resilience amidst prolonged geopolitical stress.

feed.title: Travelers Stock: Do Analysts See Meaningful Upside in 2026?
feed.summary: Travelers Companies (TRV) posted strong full-year 2025 results, with core income up 26% year-over-year and after-tax underlying underwriting income nearly tripling since 2022. Analysts recognize the company's improved profitability and AI-driven efficiency initiatives. While the consensus price target implies modest upside, TIKR's valuation model suggests a higher price target based on sustained net income margins and revenue growth, provided operating targets are met.

feed.title: Ramaco Resources authorizes internal reorganization into four divisions to enhance financing flexibility
feed.summary: Ramaco Resources has approved an internal reorganization to create four distinct business divisions. This move is aimed at enhancing financing flexibility and improving investor transparency. The divisions will initially be 100% owned subsidiaries, allowing for tailored financing strategies and potential separate public market access.

feed.title: MKDWELL Tech (NASDAQ: MKDW) adopts BVI home-country governance exemptions
feed.summary: MKDWELL Tech Inc. (NASDAQ: MKDW) has announced its decision to adopt British Virgin Islands (BVI) home-country governance practices, opting out of certain Nasdaq shareholder approval rules. As a foreign private issuer, the company will use the Nasdaq Rule 5615(a)(3) exemption to bypass shareholder approval requirements for stock or asset acquisitions, changes of control, equity-based compensation, and large private issuances that would typically require such approval for U.S. domestic Nasdaq-listed companies. BVI counsel has confirmed these practices are permitted, and MKDWELL Tech states its other corporate governance practices remain materially similar to those of U.S. domestic Nasdaq companies.

feed.title: Stryker names Nick Mead Vice President, Investor Relations
feed.summary: Stryker, a global medical technologies leader, announced the appointment of Nick Mead as Vice President, Investor Relations, effective May 1, 2026. Mead brings nearly 20 years of experience at Stryker in various leadership and finance roles. Concurrently, Jason Beach, the former VP of Finance and Investor Relations, has been named Vice President, Group Chief Financial Officer, MedSurg and Neurotechnology.

feed.title: Stryker names Nick Mead as VP of investor relations By Investing.com
feed.summary: Stryker has appointed Nick Mead as its new Vice President of Investor Relations, effective May 1, 2026. Mead, who has been with Stryker for nearly 20 years, previously served as VP of Finance for the Medical division and brings extensive financial expertise to the role. This leadership change comes as the company's stock trades near its 52-week low, though InvestingPro analysis suggests it may be undervalued.

feed.title: Ford Is A Compelling Deep Value Play - Initiating With A Buy (NYSE:F)
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company (F) has been initiated with a buy rating, despite recent operational weaknesses, due to its deep value and long-term potential. The company's full-year guidance projects a significant adjusted EBIT increase, supported by a strategy focusing on affordable vehicles, commercial AI innovation, and a new share buyback program. A favorable risk/reward profile is indicated by its forward P/E of 7.3, making it attractive for long-term investors.

feed.title: Can monday.com’s (MNDY) Agentalent.ai Marketplace Deepen Its Upmarket AI Positioning?
feed.summary: monday.com has launched Agentalent.ai, a marketplace for enterprises to hire AI agents, aiming to solidify its position in the upmarket and AI sector. This initiative supports monday.com’s strategy to deepen its role within larger enterprises and justify its significant AI investments, despite ongoing risks related to customer acquisition and profitability in the SMB segment. The company projects substantial revenue and earnings growth by 2028, with current valuations suggesting a significant upside.

feed.title: Metalpha Technology (NASDAQ: MATH) director Lin Sen files initial Form 3 ownership
feed.summary: Metalpha Technology (NASDAQ: MATH) director Lin Sen has filed an initial Form 3, which is a required disclosure for insiders regarding their share ownership. This filing indicates Lin Sen's new status as a director but does not report any transactions, share holdings, or derivative positions. The Form 3 serves as an initial statement of beneficial ownership, fulfilling regulatory requirements without disclosing any stock purchases or sales.

feed.title: [6-K] Maase Inc. Current Report (Foreign Issuer) | MAAS SEC Filing - Form 6-K
feed.summary: Maase Inc. (MAAS) has completed its acquisition of Times Good Limited, which controls the Huazhi Group, a prominent computing power and AI algorithm provider in China, for approximately RMB1.1 billion. This strategic move aims to transform Maase from a "Scenario Operator" to an "AI Industry Player" with vertically integrated, full-stack AI capabilities. The acquisition involved the issuance of 87,400,144 Class A ordinary shares and a deferred cash payment of US$26 million, with the sellers collectively holding about 19.77% of Maase's share capital and subject to a 60-month lock-up period.

feed.title: MAAS closes Huazhi deal to add AI computing and algorithms
feed.summary: MAAS (NASDAQ: MAAS) has completed its acquisition of 100% equity in Times Good Limited, which controls Huazhi Future, on March 30, 2026. This strategic move aims to transform MAAS into a full-stack, self-controlled AI industry player by integrating computing infrastructure, algorithms, hardware, and services. The market reacted positively to the news, with MAAS stock gaining 9.90%.

feed.title: Metalpha (NASDAQ: MATH) CEO details warrants, RSUs and share stakes
feed.summary: Metalpha Technology Holding Ltd's CEO, Wang Bingzhong, has filed an initial ownership report (Form 3) detailing his equity stakes in the company. The report outlines his direct and indirect holdings, which include ordinary shares, warrants, and Restricted Share Units (RSUs), providing a baseline of his interests in Metalpha. These holdings consist of a warrant for 1,200,000 ordinary shares, various unvested RSUs, and direct/indirect ownership of millions of ordinary shares, some attributed to his spouse and a spouse-controlled entity.

feed.title: SK hynix Orders Hybrid Bonding Equipment From Applied Materials and Besi
feed.summary: SK hynix has ordered a hybrid bonding inline system, jointly developed by Applied Materials and Besi, for the development of next-generation high-bandwidth memory (HBM). This is SK hynix's first mass-production hybrid bonding equipment purchase, indicating preparation for future HBM technologies, likely for HBMs beyond the immediate next generation. The system, estimated at 20 billion won, integrates chemical mechanical polishing, plasma processing, and a hybrid die bonder to enable direct copper-to-copper bonding, improving integration density and performance.
feed.source: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media
feed.source_domain: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media

feed.title: Radiation Oncology Treatment Planning Software Market Report 2026: Siemens Healthineers, Koninklijke Philips, and GE Healthcare Technologies Lead
feed.summary: The global radiation oncology treatment planning software market is projected to grow from $2.51 billion in 2025 to $3.85 billion by 2030, driven by increasing cancer incidence, advanced imaging technologies, and the expansion of oncology centers. Key players like Siemens Healthineers, Koninklijke Philips, and GE Healthcare Technologies are leading this expansion, with significant growth potential identified in the Asia-Pacific region and a focus on personalized treatment and AI integration. Innovations in targeting tumors more precisely and minimizing side effects are crucial drivers for this market.

feed.title: Ramaco Resources, Inc. Announces Internal Corporate Reorganization
feed.summary: Ramaco Resources, Inc. is undergoing a strategic internal corporate reorganization to align its corporate structure with its distinct business activities and asset portfolios. The reorganization aims to maximize shareholder value by enhancing operational focus, improving transparency, and facilitating future financing opportunities for its various business divisions. The company's operations will be divided into four principal operating divisions: Metallurgical Coal Production and Sales Operations, Rare Earth and Critical Mineral Development and Sales Operations, Royalty and Infrastructure, and Critical Mineral Refining and Processing.

feed.title: Here Are Tuesday’s Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: Arista Networks, Block, Colgate-Palmolive, Digital Realty, Emerson Electric, MiniMed, Shake Shack, and More
feed.summary: This article summarizes top Wall Street analyst upgrades, downgrades, and initiations for several companies on Tuesday, March 31, 2026. Key companies include Arista Networks, Block, Colgate-Palmolive, Digital Realty, Emerson Electric, MiniMed, and Shake Shack. The report also provides a market overview influenced by geopolitical events and oil prices, affecting major indices, treasury bonds, commodities, and cryptocurrency markets.

feed.title: UnitedHealth, Morgan Stanley, Blackstone: CNBC’s ‘Final Trades’ - Allstate (NYSE:ALL), Blackstone (NYSE:B
feed.summary: This article summarizes the "Final Trades" discussed on CNBC, featuring stock picks and financial news for UnitedHealth Group, Morgan Stanley, Blackstone Inc., and The Allstate Corporation. UnitedHealth is set to release its Q1 earnings, while Morgan Stanley recently surpassed $1 trillion in IRA assets. Blackstone led a consortium to acquire the Royal Challengers Bengaluru cricket franchise for $1.78 billion, and Allstate is anticipated to report significantly higher Q1 earnings.

feed.title: Ariel Global Fund Initiated a Position in Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (HPE) in Q4
feed.summary: Ariel Global Fund initiated a position in Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (HPE) in Q4 2025, as revealed in their latest investor letter. Despite HPE's recent acquisition of Juniper Networks and potential catalysts from AI-driven infrastructure and networking demand, the stock has lagged due to cautious guidance and inconsistent execution. Ariel Investments sees an attractive long-term opportunity in HPE, believing its strategic moves and potential for growth outweigh short-term sentiment.

feed.title: TD Cowen Adjusts Price Target on Church & Dwight to $112 From $93, Maintains Hold Rating
feed.summary: TD Cowen has increased its price target for Church & Dwight to $112 from $93, while maintaining a Hold rating on the stock. This adjustment comes after other analysts like Oppenheimer and Wells Fargo also recently raised their price targets for the household products company. Church & Dwight specializes in cleaning, hygiene, and personal care products, with 82% of its net sales coming from the United States.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts State Street Price Target to $155 From $172, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for State Street Corporation (STT) to $155 from $172, while retaining an "Overweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst various financial news updates concerning State Street, including their ex-dividend date announcement and approvals for stake buys in Groww Asset Management by State Street Global Advisors. The article also provides a company profile, financial data, and analyst consensus for State Street.

feed.title: Lulu’s Fashion Lounge Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:LVLU) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Lulu’s Fashion Lounge Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:LVLU) reported its Q4 2025 earnings, exceeding expectations with an EPS of $-0.14 against an anticipated $-1.35. The company highlighted a "transformational year" in 2025, marked by strengthening its financial foundation, improving operational efficiencies, and achieving three consecutive quarters of positive adjusted EBITDA. They are strategically focusing on enhancing casual and footwear categories, expanding wholesale presence, and leveraging technology to drive engagement and efficiency, aiming for continued profitability and growth in 2026.

feed.title: Ariel Global Fund Exited Eversource Energy (ES) After Operational Headwinds and Weakening Key Indicators
feed.summary: Ariel Global Fund, managed by Ariel Investments, exited its position in Eversource Energy (NYSE: ES) during the fourth quarter of 2025. The fund had initially invested in the regulated utility company anticipating operational stability after asset divestitures and a major offshore wind project. However, the decision to exit was made due to weakening key indicators and continued operational headwinds.

feed.title: TD Cowen Adjusts Price Target on Clorox to $111 From $98, Maintains Hold Rating
feed.summary: TD Cowen has adjusted its price target for Clorox (CLX) to $111, an increase from its previous target of $98. The firm has concurrently maintained its "Hold" rating on the stock. This update reflects a revised outlook from the analyst firm on the consumer products company.

feed.title: Waste Management (NYSE: WM) details 2026 proxy, board and ESPP vote
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) has released details for its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, scheduled for May 12, 2026, in Houston. Shareholders will vote on electing nine directors, ratifying Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor, an advisory resolution on executive compensation, and an amendment to the Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP) to increase authorized shares. The proxy statement also covers WM's executive compensation philosophy, including performance-based incentives and stock ownership guidelines, and board oversight of risk and sustainability.

feed.title: Hershey’s Growth Strategy Leans Into Salty, Better-for-You Snacks
feed.summary: Hershey is expanding its growth strategy beyond traditional chocolate to focus on high-growth snacking categories. The company plans to invest in premium, better-for-you, salty, and functional snacks while also modernizing its supply chain. This move includes combining sweet, salty, and protein brand portfolios to streamline marketing and distribution efforts.

feed.title: Argus Research Adjusts Corteva Price Target to $91 From $80, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Argus Research has increased its price target for Corteva (CTVA) to $91 from $80, while reaffirming a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook on the agricultural chemical and seed company. The article also notes other recent analyst adjustments and news related to Corteva and the broader agricultural industry.

feed.title: Jefferies reiterates Savara stock Buy rating on approval confidence
feed.summary: Jefferies has reiterated a Buy rating and $10.50 price target for Savara (NASDAQ:SVRA) due to high confidence in the regulatory approval of its Molbreevi treatment for autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis lung disease. The firm projects $500 million in U.S. peak sales and a potential EU approval by Q1 2027, with the stock currently trading at $5.05 after a 77% return over the past year. Regulatory hurdles have been addressed, and both the FDA and EMA are progressing with Molbreevi's applications without an advisory committee for the former.

feed.title: Oracle Empowers Defense Industrial Base to Innovate at Scale with New Isolated Cloud
feed.summary: Oracle has launched a new Defense Industrial Base Isolated Cloud Environment, designed to enable secure collaboration for defense contractors at Secret and Top-Secret levels. This environment will allow for secure classified research and development, system integration, and mission support within air-gapped Oracle National Security Regions. The initiative aims to accelerate program timelines and enhance mission effectiveness while ensuring compliance with Department of War security standards.

feed.title: President El-Sisi meets Apache Corp.'s CEO and delegation
feed.summary: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi met with Apache Corporation's CEO, Mr. John Christmann, along with other officials, to discuss the company's operations and expansion plans in Egypt. El-Sisi affirmed Egypt's commitment to supporting Apache's activities and ensuring the optimal utilization of investment opportunities in the oil and gas sector. Apache’s CEO highlighted the company's nearly three-decade partnership with Egypt, $5 billion in investments over the last five years, and intentions for further investment and exploration, with the Minister of Petroleum praising Apache as the largest American investor in Egypt.

feed.title: Oracle Unveils AI Data Platform for US Federal Government
feed.summary: Oracle has launched its AI Data Platform for US federal agencies, providing a secure and unified foundation to integrate generative AI models with agency data and applications. This platform, leveraging Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, Autonomous AI Database, and OCI Enterprise AI, aims to help federal agencies accelerate AI adoption, streamline data management, and enhance decision-making through real-time insights and automated workflows. It is designed to meet stringent federal security and compliance requirements, including FedRAMP High authorization and options for data sovereignty.

feed.title: KORE Group (NYSE:KORE) Issues Earnings Results
feed.summary: KORE Group (NYSE:KORE) reported a significant earnings miss for the quarter, with an EPS of ($2.84) against an expected ($0.53), a $2.31 shortfall. However, the company's revenue of $73.86 million slightly beat analysts' estimates of $70.50 million. Despite the stock trading near its 52-week high, Wall Street analysts maintain a "Reduce" consensus rating with a $4.00 target price.

feed.title: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.06 EPS
feed.summary: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) reported strong Q2 earnings, beating analyst expectations with EPS of $4.46 and revenue of $611.02 million. The company announced a $600 million share buyback and a quarterly dividend of $1.10, indicating a commitment to shareholder returns. Despite a generally "Reduce" average analyst rating, FactSet showed resilient subscription growth and strategic advancements, including an AI-native workflow solution.

feed.title: NVIDIA AI Ecosystem Expands as Marvell Joins Forces Through NVLink Fusion
feed.summary: NVIDIA and Marvell Technology have announced a strategic partnership to integrate Marvell into the NVIDIA AI factory and AI-RAN ecosystem through NVLink Fusion. This collaboration aims to provide greater choice and flexibility for customers developing next-generation AI infrastructure, with NVIDIA also investing $2 billion in Marvell. The companies will work together on custom XPUs, scale-up networking, silicon photonics technology, and transforming telecommunication networks into AI infrastructure.

feed.title: Biogen to buy Apellis Pharmaceuticals for $5.6 billion to expand its rare disease portfolio
feed.summary: Biogen announced its intent to acquire Apellis Pharmaceuticals for $5.6 billion in cash, aiming to bolster its rare disease portfolio. This acquisition includes Apellis' drugs Empaveli, used for rare kidney diseases and a blood disorder, and Syfovre, which treats an advanced eye disorder. The deal offers Apellis shareholders $41 per share plus potential future payments based on Syfovre's global sales milestones.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ALERT Bernstein Liebhard LLP Announces A
feed.summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP has announced a securities class action lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) on behalf of investors who purchased shares between February 4, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Gartner made misrepresentations regarding its contract value growth rate. Shareholders who wish to serve as lead plaintiff must file papers by May 18, 2026.

feed.title: Ford’s $7.7 Billion Pro Forma EBIT Case: Why $20 Target Price Holds
feed.summary: Ford's FY2025 adjusted EBIT of $6.8 billion was significantly impacted by non-recurring headwinds, hiding an underlying performance closer to $7.7 billion. With ambitious 2026 guidance, strong cost reductions, and strategic moves like the Universal EV Platform, Ford is positioning itself for substantial margin expansion. TIKR's valuation model suggests a mid-case target price of $19.61 by December 2030, indicating a significant upside from current levels as the market underprices Ford's operational improvements.
